text: Hello guys, my name is Nitesh and you are welcome to my YouTube channel. So, if you remember, in the last video I made an announcement that I am going to start a deep learning playlist on my channel. So, that will happen today. Today we will start our 100 days of deep learning course. This will be the first video. And before starting the video, let me give you a little introduction that what we will cover in this video. So, since we are just starting and I have decided that this course will be beginner friendly. So, the idea is very simple that we will start from scratch and gradually move to complex topics. So, today is day 1 and I want you to know everything about deep learning. So, that is why the title of today's video is what is deep learning. So, if you are a beginner and entering this field for the first time, then by watching this video you will understand what is deep learning and what is its origin. Apart from this, one more topic that I have covered in this video is that what is the difference between machine learning and deep learning. So, I will tell you the differences on 4-5 different points, both theoretical and practical aspects. And after that, I am covering one more topic in this video that why deep learning has become so famous now. Means what are the reasons behind the success of deep learning. I am teaching this topic only because I want that when you enter the field of deep learning, then you have a complete perspective that why things are the way they are and what are the reasons behind it. So, that is why in this video, if you watch this video completely, then you will understand the whole landscape of deep learning in today's date, in 2022. And this video will prepare you very well for the future. So, I think this introduction is enough. Let's start the video. So, guys, let's start from the beginning. First of all, I will tell you what is deep learning. And I will give you the definition of deep learning. So, if you go on the internet and type what is deep learning, then you will mostly see two types of definitions. One will be a simple version and the other will be a technical definition. In this video, I will tell you both the definitions. Because both will help you in your understanding. So, our first definition is on your screen. Where it is written that deep learning is a subfield of artificial intelligence and machine learning that is inspired by the structure of a human brain. Also, deep learning algorithms attempt to draw similar conclusions as humans would by continually analyzing data with a given logical structure called neural network. So, there are two parts in this definition. Let's break down and focus on the first line. So, from the first line, we understand that deep learning is a subfield of AI and machine learning. So, this diagram will help you a little. In this diagram, it is clearly visible that the biggest umbrella term in which everything comes is artificial intelligence. What is artificial intelligence? It is basically the instinct of human beings where we want to make intelligent machines like us. We have been working on this for years, maybe 100 years. And some developments have also taken place. Among which, the biggest development is machine learning. So, machine learning is basically a subfield of AI where you learn from data. So, if you have data, then if there is input and output in that data, then the job of your machine learning algorithm is to figure out the relationship between input and output. So, machine learning is a subfield of AI. Now, what is deep learning? Deep learning is a subfield of machine learning where you are doing everything like machine learning. But the biggest difference between machine learning and deep learning is that machine learning depends on statistical techniques. To capture this relationship between input and output, most of the machine learning algorithms focus on statistical techniques. Whereas, deep learning depends on a logical structure which we call neural network. And this neural network is inspired by the human brain. Computer scientist said that if we want to make intelligent things, then let's get inspired by the most intelligent thing on earth, human beings. The way human beings think, the way they make decisions, what if we make algorithms that can map that thinking. So, neural network is basically a logical structure which is inspired by human brain. And this collection of algorithms is collectively called deep learning. Now, I will show you an example of a deep learning neural network. This is the example. This is a neural network. This is a simple neural network which we call ANN. Artificial Neural Network. Now, we are going to study this in detail. Let me give you a brief introduction. So, Artificial Neural Network is basically a logical structure where the fundamental unit is called Perceptron. All these circles you can see are Perceptrons. And they are connected to each other with these arrows. And these arrows are called weights. And these are called layers. These are all layers. All the Perceptrons in one line are called layers. These are all layers. Now, you give input from here. And you get output from here. I gave you a very simple description. Now, you call the input layer from where you get input. And you call the output layer from where you get output. Now, you can add as many layers as you want in between. There are two layers here but you can add as many layers as you want. These are called hidden layers. In fact, this is a fun fact. The name of deep learning comes from the number of hidden layers you have. If there are a lot of hidden layers in the middle, then this network becomes deep. So, that is why this name is deep learning. Now, you must be wondering whether we have only one type of neural network or there are different types. So, the answer is that there are different types of neural networks. This is just one type. There is CNN. The simplest one. Then there is CNN. Convolutional Neural Network. Then there is RNN. Recurrent Neural Network. Apart from this, there are many other types of neural networks which solve different purposes. For example, Convolutional Neural Networks work well on speech data. Similarly, RNN works well on speech and textual data. GAN can generate things. It can generate text and images. It is very interesting. In this way, you will get to read many more neural networks in this course. On the basis of the discussion till now, I will summarize. Deep learning is a subfield of machine learning. Machine learning works in the same way as deep learning. It just works in a different way. The work is to find the relationship between input and output. Mostly, in supervised learning. Machine learning uses statistical techniques to work. Whereas, deep learning follows a logical structure which is called a neural network. These neural networks are designed in such a way that they try to replicate the human brain. Why did the computer scientists think so? Because they felt that if you want to make intelligent machines, then why not copy the human brain which is the most advanced intelligence form on earth. Now, ANN looks like what I described to you. It is the simplest neural network. We also understood that apart from this, there are many other neural networks which we will gradually study in this course. Now, everything is clear till here. Now, the question arises and it should also come to your mind that why is deep learning getting so famous? Recently, you must have noticed that deep learning is being heard and seen everywhere. What is the reason? What has deep learning done because of which deep learning is getting so much fame? Let's discuss that. There are two major reasons behind deep learning's fame. The first reason is applicability. I will explain what it means. The second reason is performance. The meaning of applicability is that the deep learning algorithms are applicable in a very large domain. You won't believe that deep learning works in many areas like computer vision, speech recognition, natural language processing, machine translation, bioinformatics, drug design, medical image analysis, climate science, material inspection, board games programs. You can apply deep learning in any of these fields. The second reason is performance. The performance of deep learning in all these fields is exploding. In fact, deep learning has provided the best state-of-the-art results. You can't surpass such performance. In fact, in some scenarios, deep learning has beaten the performance of human experts. You must have heard of a famous Chinese game called Go where an AI agent called AlphaGo defeated the world champion of Go 4 times in 5 games. There are many such scenarios where you will see that the state-of-the-art performance of deep learning is coming and it is leaving the human experts behind. This is the reason because of which deep learning is getting so much attention from the software and computer science community. You will see many examples like self-driving car, new drugs are being made in medical science, new research in the field of chemistry is driven by deep learning. In the next video, I will tell you about different types of applications where deep learning has left its mark. The sudden fame of deep learning has come due to two things. First, deep learning algorithms are designed to be applied to a wide domain of problems. Second, performance. Wherever it is being applied, their performance remains state-of-the-art. At some places, it is surpassing human experts. Due to this, a reputation of deep learning is being built. I hope you understood a little about deep learning. Now let's move ahead in this video. As I said in the beginning, I will tell you two definitions. We have seen the first definition which was a very common definition. Now let's move on to a slightly technical definition. The definition is on your screen. It is written here that deep learning is a part of a broader family of machine learning methods based on artificial neural networks with representation learning. It is also written that deep learning algorithms uses multiple layers to progressively extract higher-level features from the raw input. For example, in image processing, lower layers may identify edges while higher layers may identify the concepts relevant to a human such as digits or letters or faces. Again, there are two parts in this definition. Let's focus on the first one. In the first definition, it is said that it comes under machine learning and is based on artificial neural networks. But here one more word is written representation learning. According to this definition, deep learning depends on representation learning. Now the question is what is representation learning? Trust me, if you want to get into deep learning, you should have an understanding of representation learning. In fact, let me show you. This is the Wikipedia page of representation learning. It is written here In machine learning, feature learning and representation learning are a set of techniques that allow a system to automatically discover the representations needed for feature detection or classification from raw data. This replaces manual feature engineering and allows a machine to both learn the features and use them to perform a specific task. Let me explain this in easy words. If you have worked a little in machine learning, then you must know that there is a thing called feature extraction or you can also call it feature engineering. What do you do in that process? What features do you create? Let me give you an example. Let's say you want to make a dog-cat classifier where if you are given an image, then you have to look at that image and tell whether it has a dog or a cat. If you go to solve this problem with machine learning, then you will have to create manual features. How can features be? For example, size is a feature. If the size is big, then it is a dog. If the size is small, then it is a cat. Color can be a feature. All the physical attributes can become features. But you have to understand one thing here that in machine learning, you have to create these features for your machine learning algorithm. But what happens in deep learning is that this whole process is automated. That means you don't make such rules. You just provide the image in the right format and your deep learning algorithm automatically extracts features on its intelligence. This is called representation learning where automatic feature extraction happens from your problem, from your data. This is one more thing to understand and we will discuss it further that there is no need for feature engineering in deep learning. It automatically does your algorithm from your data. Now if you move to the second part of the definition, then it is written here that deep learning algorithm uses multiple layers. We have already seen this. To progressively extract higher level features from raw input. And then an example is given. I will explain the same example to you. This part of the definition tells a very important thing about deep learning algorithms that deep learning algorithms are basically neural networks. There are layers in neural networks. Now there is a purpose of these layers. For example, if we take this example of dog-cat classification, then what will happen is you will feed your image data from here. You will feed your data pixel by pixel here. Now there is a purpose of each layer. Obviously, you will get the output here that it is a dog or a cat. But these middle layers also have a purpose. So the initial layers will extract some primitive features from your data. For example, if you are doing cat-dog analysis, then these first layers detect edges. Then if you go to the next layer, then it will detect some more complex features like shapes. Edges are detected like this. Shapes are detected from the next layer. And then the more you go ahead, the more complex features you will make from them. For example, faces. If you read it, it is written here. In image processing, lower layers may identify edges while higher layers may identify the concepts relevant to a human such as digits or letters or faces. So slowly you identify things from here. So basic things in the beginning and gradually complex things and eventually you give your result in the last layer. This definition is explaining two things to you. I will summarize it again. First, deep learning uses representation learning. Which means that you don't have to extract features. The machine will do it by itself. The algorithm will do it by itself. From the second part of the definition, you learned that what is the way to work on it. The way to work on it is that you give data in the input layer and then through the hidden layers you start detecting things. The hidden layers in the beginning extract primitive features and the deep layers in the end extract complex features. And that is how this thing works. Now if I wanted, I could have skipped this part. But I felt that you should have this idea in the beginning because we are going to discuss it in detail later. So guys, now we have an idea about what deep learning is. Now we will discuss what is the point of difference between deep learning and machine learning. Till this point, we only know that deep learning is a sub-field of machine learning. The algorithms of machine learning mostly use statistical techniques to work. Whereas deep learning is a logical structure based on the human brain which we call neural networks. Apart from that, we are going to discuss the other point of differences at the practicality level. First of all, I have written 5 points here. These are the 5 points we are going to talk about. The first point of difference between deep learning and machine learning is that deep learning needs a lot of data compared to machine learning. If you are working on a problem, then you can only apply deep learning when you have a lot of data. Somehow, deep learning needs a lot of data to be sure about things. After seeing a lot of data, your performance of deep learning models becomes reliable. If you see here, there is a graph where on the x-axis is the amount of data available for training and on the y-axis is the performance of the model. If you take this point in this graph, let's say you have this much data i.e. less data, then you can see that the performance of machine learning models is better than deep learning models. For less data, for less data, ML wins. But as you get more data than this amount, there are two things to see. The first thing is that the performance of machine learning models stagnates. If you are adding more data after one point, then the performance of the model is not improving. Whereas, if you follow the curve of deep learning, then you can see that with the increase of data, your performance is improving almost linearly. The thing to understand here is that deep learning is data hungry. As the data increases, linearly, performance also increases. This is the first difference. The second big difference is hardware. If you want to train machine learning models, then you can use CPU on your simple machine. But deep learning models have very complex matrix multiplication going on. A lot of numbers. To handle this, you need GPU. Graphical Processing Unit. Without it, if you run your deep learning models on CPU, then your deep learning model will run slow. You need powerful GPUs with more memory where you can do matrix multiplication calculations. Hardware is costly in deep learning. The second point of difference is that machine learning can run on cheap hardware but to train deep learning models, you need costly hardware. You need costly hardware for GPU. Graphical Processing Unit. The third point is training time. Again, deep learning models are complex and that is why your model training time is high. Training time is very high in comparison to machine learning where the training time is low. In fact, if you apply deep learning algorithm on a large dataset, I have heard that people take weeks to train the model. I have also heard that some researchers take months for a specific purpose. To train a model in machine learning model, you hardly need minutes. Sometimes it may happen that you have to run the model for hours. I have not heard that a weak machine learning model is being trained. Training time is very high in deep learning. If you talk about prediction time, then prediction time in deep learning is very fast. You will understand why it is fast later. Whereas, if you talk about machine learning algorithms, then in some algorithms, prediction time is very slow. For example, it takes a lot of time to predict in KNN type algorithms. So, it varies for machine learning. Prediction time varies. However, training time is definitely less for machine learning and more for deep learning. Next point is feature selection. We have already discussed this point. We discussed that there is a thing called representation learning. We discussed this a while ago. We learned that deep learning algorithms automatically extract relevant features from the data. Whereas, if you are working with machine learning, then you have to manually extract those features. Let me give you an example. A year ago, we were making a resume based project. It was a resume based project. Let me give you an example. We had to predict based on the content of the resume whether the child will get a placement or not. So that we can give him some feedback. Now, when you apply a deep learning approach, you have to extract features from the resume. One feature can be marks of 12th standard. Second feature can be marks of 10th standard. Third feature can be number of achievements. Another feature can be number of courses. Another feature can be quality of college. You can make all these features and then you can predict whether the child will get a placement or not. But obviously, to make this feature, we need domain experts. I have to sit with some HRs or some teachers who will tell me to make such features. Depending on the domain. But if you do this project through deep learning, then you just have to provide the algorithm with the content of the resume in the right form. Behind the scenes, it will automatically extract features and it will give you the prediction. This is called representation learning and this is a big benefit when you use deep learning. In fact, I gave you an example that if you have a neural network and you are giving it an image, then the initial layers extract simple features and primitive features and then complex features are automatically extracted. I have a diagram of this. If you are giving this photograph, then the initial layers, the first and second hidden layer, will extract simple features like edges. Then in the middle, some middle features like shapes will be detected. You can see these shapes are being detected and by doing this, eventually in the last, high level features are detected like a complete face. This is how deep learning works. This feature extraction is happening automatically. I am not doing anything. The network is designed in such a way that everything is happening automatically. How it is happening, we will discuss later. This is also a big point of difference. The last point is interpretability. What happens is, we just discussed that feature extraction is happening automatically. The features are extracted automatically. I don't know what that feature is. I don't know which edges are there. I don't understand it. What happens is, the model that comes to you is not interpretable. If I make a dog versus cat classifier, in such an architecture, then as soon as I give it an image, it will tell me whether it is a dog or a cat. I have already told you how it will work. The first layer will find some primitive features. The next layer will find some complex features. Internally, whatever happens, it will be like a black box. It will not tell me what it saw in that image. It means that if someone asks me tomorrow that why did you call this image a dog, I don't have any answer. I don't have any answers. This is an issue of interpretability. This is a problem. Suppose you are training a deep learning model on a social networking website where you can ban a user by looking at the comments. If you use deep learning, it will read the comment and decide whether to ban or not. Let's say it says ban this user and you ban it. The user will come and ask you that why did you ban me? Tell me the reason. But since you have a deep learning model, you won't know the reason why your algorithm banned it. That is a big flaw. Explainability is important in many scenarios. Interpretability is important in deep learning. Deep learning fails there. But if you compare it with machine learning, then interpretability is very high in machine learning. Let's say you are training a linear model, a logistic regression model where CGPA is one column IQ is another column and placement is the third column. When you train a logistic regression model, there will be two weights W1 and W2. W1 is the weightage of this feature and W2 is the weightage of this feature. Based on the value of W1 and W2, I can tell you which feature is more important and which is less important. If a child comes and asks why I was told that my placement won't happen, then I will say since your W1 is very less, your CGPA is very less, that is why your placement is less. In machine learning models, there is a proper decision making and you get a flowchart like this. This flowchart can tell you why a point is called 1 and why a point is called 0. The explainability or you can say interpretability is quite high. It helps in those scenarios where you have to tell the other person the reason for giving you 1 or 0. These are the 5 points of differences. Let's summarize. Data Dependency. There is a lot of deep learning and less of machine learning. The good thing is that as the data increases, its performance scales linearly. If we talk about hardware, deep learning lags behind. Why? Because it needs hardware, GPU to handle complex matrix multiplications. This is against deep learning. If we talk about training time, deep learning is slow. It takes a lot of time to train models in comparison to machine learning. Prediction time is fast. Feature selection, I have already told you. Representation learning follows. Features are generated automatically. What was the matter of layers? The initial layers find small features, the final layers find complex features. You understood all this. Interpretability. Deep learning is a black box. Only it knows what it thinks and decides. We can't know. If we are working in a scenario where we have to tell the result why we reached that result, then deep learning is not a good choice. These are the 4-5 things. I guess after reading and understanding these 4-5 things, you will reach a conclusion that machine learning cannot be replaced by deep learning. Many people think that if deep learning is so good and powerful, then why not use deep learning in every project? The reason is that in some things, machine learning is better than deep learning. You must have heard an old saying that where there is a need for a needle, swords are not used. Machine learning is a needle and deep learning is a sword. Your purpose is to steal clothes or to kill people. Depending on that, you will select whether you need machine learning or deep learning. I hope you understood this section. We have read many things regarding deep learning. Now we will answer some questions. When I was studying deep learning, I was getting a question in my mind that why is deep learning becoming so famous now? I will teach you the history of deep learning after 1-2 videos. There you will notice that the work on deep learning started in 1960s from the time of Alan Turing. But it became famous now. Around 2012 is called breakthrough year. So what happened after 2010 that deep learning became such a powerful and famous technology. If you want to answer this question, there are multiple reasons. So the answer of why now can be different. I will tell you one by one. The first reason is data sets. We will talk about this later. The second reason is frameworks. The third reason is architecture of models. The fourth reason is hardware. And the last reason is community. We will talk about these 5 points. This will give you some perspective about deep learning and the driving forces behind it. Let's start with the first one. Data sets. I have already told you that deep learning is data hungry. It is data hungry. It needs a lot of data to make its predictions. It needs a lot of data to make sure about the patterns in the data. If you talk about machine learning, if you have a dataset of 100 rows or 1000 rows, then you can train and build a model on it. But not with deep learning. Deep learning needs lakhs of rows. Then it will work. The good thing is that around 2010 two revolutions came in our society. First came smartphone revolution and second, parallelly internet pricing revolution. I would say. Because internet was already there. Prices have reduced a lot. Around 2015-16, Reliance entered the Jio market and started providing high speed internet for free. If you combine these two things, then everyone in the world has a smartphone with high speed internet connection. People now use different apps.
